Abstract

This Policy defines the requirements for protecting <CLIENTNAME>’s information assets and resources from physical and environmental threats in order to reduce the risk of loss, theft, damage or unauthorized access

**STATEMENT OF CONFIDENTIALITY**

All information in this document is <CLIENTNAME> PRIVILEGED and for INTERNAL USE ONLY.

The information in this document may contain <CLIENTNAME> proprietary trade secrets and confidential information, the disclosure of which could substantially benefit competitors. As a result, this document should not be disclosed, used or duplicated – in whole or in part without the explicit authorization of <CLIENTNAME> management.

The information is intended solely for the use of authorized recipients. If you are not the intended recipient, you are prohibited from reading, using, disseminating, distributing and copying this document.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_3
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 1 INTRODUCTION
text:
1 INTRODUCTION

Physical and environmental security refers to measures taken to protect an organization’s information systems, facilities, and related infrastructure from threats against the physical environment. Physical and environmental safeguards are often overlooked, but are very important in protecting information. Facilities that house information and Information Technology (IT) resources shall be afforded appropriate protection to avoid damage or unauthorized access to information and systems. In addition, equipment that store information shall be physically protected. <CLIENTNAME> is committed to providing effective physical and environmental protection mechanisms in order to safeguard information assets from unauthorized disclosure and damage as well as other environmental calamities. 

1 INTRODUCTION

Physical and environmental security refers to measures taken to protect an organization’s information systems, facilities, and related infrastructure from threats against the physical environment. Physical and environmental safeguards are often overlooked, but are very important in protecting information. Facilities that house information and Information Technology (IT) resources shall be afforded appropriate protection to avoid damage or unauthorized access to information and systems. In addition, equipment that store information shall be physically protected. <CLIENTNAME> is committed to providing effective physical and environmental protection mechanisms in order to safeguard information assets from unauthorized disclosure and damage as well as other environmental calamities.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_4
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 2 PURPOSE
text:
2 PURPOSE
The purpose of this Physical and Environmental Security Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that physical access to organization’s premises is controlled, monitored and security measures are implemented to safeguard its users, assets and premises against physical security risks and environmental hazards.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal laws and regulations including but not limited to ISO 27001 and UAE Information Assurance (IA) Regulation.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
        2.1 Definition of security perimeters: Definition and use of security perimeters to protect areas that contain either sensitive or critical information, or information processing facilities.
        2.2 Application of physical and environmental protection: Design and implementation of physical and environmental protection for secure areas and guidelines for working in secure areas.

2 PURPOSE

The purpose of this Physical and Environmental Security Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that physical access to organization’s premises is controlled, monitored and security measures are implemented to safeguard its users, assets and premises against physical security risks and environmental hazards.

Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal laws and regulations including but not limited to ISO 27001 and UAE Information Assurance (IA) Regulation.

The following are the key objectives of this Policy:

1. **Definition of security perimeters:** Definition and use of security perimeters to protect areas that contain either sensitive or critical information, or information processing facilities.
2. **Application of physical and environmental protection:** Design and implementation of physical and environmental protection for secure areas and guidelines for working in secure areas.

name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 3 SCOPE
text:
3 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all physical areas within <CLIENTNAME>, employees and third parties who access <CLIENTNAME> facilities, information assets and information and communications technology (ICT) systems housed within <CLIENTNAME> facilities. 

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
3 SCOPE

**Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.**

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_6
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 4 POLICY STATEMENT
text:
4 POLICY STATEMENT

<CLIENTNAME> shall implement physical and environmental security measures to ensure critical or sensitive information systems are physically protected from unauthorized access, damage, and interference. Further <CLIENTNAME> shall implement controls to protect people and equipment from physical and environmental threats.

4 POLICY STATEMENT

<CLIENTNAME> shall implement physical and environmental security measures to ensure critical or sensitive information systems are physically protected from unauthorized access, damage, and interference. Further <CLIENTNAME> shall implement controls to protect people and equipment from physical and environmental threats.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_7
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.1 Establish a Physical Security Perimeter
text:
5.1 Establish a Physical Security Perimeter
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.1.1 Identify and classify security areas based on the assets and information they process or contain.
            5.1.2 Define security perimeters, establish appropriate countermeasures such as mentioned below, and test as appropriately deemed for every classified security level to ensure the right level of protection is applied:
    a. Supervision of reception area. 
    b. Physical Barriers to company premises.
    c. Restrict access to authorized personnel and install intruder detection systems for external doors, accessible windows.
    d. Fire doors are alarmed, monitored, and tested in accordance with suitable regional, national, and international standards.
    e. Suppression systems are installed, monitored, and tested.
5.1 Establish a Physical Security Perimeter

3. **Establish a Physical Security Perimeter**

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Identify and classify security areas based on the assets and information they process or contain.
2. Define security perimeters, establish appropriate countermeasures such as mentioned below, and test as appropriately deemed for every classified security level to ensure the right level of protection is applied:
1. Physical Barriers to company premises.
2. Restrict access to authorized personnel and install intruder detection systems for external doors, accessible windows.
3. Fire doors are alarmed, monitored, and tested in accordance with suitable regional, national, and international standards.
4. Suppression systems are installed, monitored, and tested.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2
metadata.heading: 5.1 Establish a Physical Security Perimeter

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_8
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.2 Implement Physical Security Controls
text:
5.2 Implement Physical Security Controls

<CLIENTNAME> shall: 
            5.2.1 Record the date, time of entry and departure of employees/contractors/visitors and others who seek access to <CLIENTNAME> facilities.
            5.2.2 Perform physical security checks for all personnel entering the organizational premises. 
            5.2.3 Restrict the entry and usage of devices within organizational premises according to security requirements. 
            5.2.4 Supervise visitors at all times during their visit to secure areas.
            5.2.5 Verify the identity of visitors requesting access, by an appropriate means.
            5.2.6 Ensure that all employees, contractors, and external parties wear some form of visible identification within the organization’s premises.
            5.2.7 Grant access only for specific, authorized purposes to visitors and provide clear instructions on the security requirements of the area and on emergency procedures.
            5.2.8 Document and implement a process to authenticate all persons accessing secure areas. Ensure that access is provided to secure areas for only authorized personnel by implementing access controls.
            5.2.9 Ensure that access to secure areas is logged. Ensure that access logs are maintained and monitored.
            5.2.10 Ensure access rights are regularly reviewed, updated, and revoked when necessary.
            5.2.11 Ensure that sensitive personnel data (e.g., biometric data used for access controls) is protected as per applicable data privacy laws and regulations wherever required.
<CLIENTNAME> personnel shall:
            5.2.12 Always escort Contractors/third parties unless the area is explicitly designated for them with no escort requirement.
            5.2.13 Notify security personnel immediately if they encounter unescorted visitors or anyone not wearing visible identification.
5.2 Implement Physical Security Controls

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Record the date, time of entry and departure of employees/contractors/visitors and others who seek access to <CLIENTNAME> facilities.
2. Perform physical security checks for all personnel entering the organizational premises.
3. Restrict the entry and usage of devices within organizational premises according to security requirements.
4. Supervise visitors at all times during their visit to secure areas.
5. Verify the identity of visitors requesting access, by an appropriate means.
6. Ensure that all employees, contractors, and external parties wear some form of visible identification within the organization’s premises.
7. Grant access only for specific, authorized purposes to visitors and provide clear instructions on the security requirements of the area and on emergency procedures.
8. Document and implement a process to authenticate all persons accessing secure areas. Ensure that access is provided to secur

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_9
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.3 Protecting Against External and Environmental Threats
text:
5.3 Protecting Against External and Environmental Threats

<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.3.1 Provide adequate training to personnel responsible for responding to environmental and safety hazards.
            5.3.2 Ensure and monitor power, temperature and relative humidity inside data centers are in accordance with relevant regulations.
            5.3.3 Ensure data centers have 24/7 air conditioning and humidity control.
            5.3.4 Conduct planned maintenance for all supporting utilities and necessary records.
            5.3.5 Activate fire suppression system only in affected areas to limit the damage on other assets.
            5.3.6 Site equipment appropriately to prevent unauthorized access into work areas.
            5.3.7 Monitor your work environmental conditions and alert in case of a potential threat. 
5.3 Protecting Against External and Environmental Threats

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Provide adequate training to personnel responsible for responding to environmental and safety hazards.
2. Ensure and monitor power, temperature and relative humidity inside data centers are in accordance with relevant regulations.
3. Ensure data centers have 24/7 air conditioning and humidity control.
4. Conduct planned maintenance for all supporting utilities and necessary records.
5. Activate fire suppression system only in affected areas to limit the damage on other assets.
6. Site equipment appropriately to prevent unauthorized access into work areas.
7. Monitor your work environmental conditions and alert in case of a potential threat.
section: 5.3 Protecting Against External and Environmental Threats

text:
5.4 Working in Secure Areas

<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.4.1 Establish guidelines for working in secure areas.
            5.4.2 Make sure all personnel accessing secure areas are aware of and accept organization’s rules and guidelines.

5.4 Working in Secure Areas

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Establish guidelines for working in secure areas.
2. Make sure all personnel accessing secure areas are aware of and accept organization’s rules and guidelines.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_11
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.5  Protect Delivery and Loading Areas
text:
5.5 Protect Delivery and Loading Areas

<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.5.1 Establish access procedures to loading and unloading areas to restrict access to only authorized personnel.
            5.5.2 Design the delivery and loading area where supplies can be loaded and unloaded without delivery personnel gaining access to other parts of the building.
            5.5.3 Establish and implement procedures for the storage of hazardous or combustible materials to reduce their risks on secure areas.
            5.5.4 Physically segregate loading and unloading activities.
<CLIENTNAME> personnel shall:
            5.5.5 Perform appropriate due diligence and implement countermeasures to securely manage incoming and outgoing materials and equipment.
5.5 Protect Delivery and Loading Areas

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Establish access procedures to loading and unloading areas to restrict access to only authorized personnel.
2. Design the delivery and loading area where supplies can be loaded and unloaded without delivery personnel gaining access to other parts of the building.
3. Establish and implement procedures for the storage of hazardous or combustible materials to reduce their risks on secure areas.
4. Physically segregate loading and unloading activities.

<CLIENTNAME> personnel shall:

1. Perform appropriate due diligence and implement countermeasures to securely manage incoming and outgoing materials and equipment.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_12
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.6 Ensure Effective Supporting Utilities
text:
5.6 Ensure Effective Supporting Utilities

<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.6.1 Ensure that each facility’s supporting utilities (e.g., electricity, telecommunications, water supply, natural gas, sewage, heating ventilation and air conditioning) are in line with local legal requirements and manufacturer’s specifications as well as tested for malfunctions.
            5.6.2 Conduct preventive maintenance for all supporting utilities and necessary records maintained for the same.
5.6 Ensure Effective Supporting Utilities

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Ensure that each facility’s supporting utilities (e.g., electricity, telecommunications, water supply, natural gas, sewage, heating ventilation and air conditioning) are in line with local legal requirements and manufacturer’s specifications as well as tested for malfunctions.
2. Conduct preventive maintenance for all supporting utilities and necessary records maintained for the same.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_13
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.7 Cabling Security
text:
5.7 Cabling Security

<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.7.1 Ensure that power and telecommunications lines into information systems are underground wherever possible.
            5.7.2 Protect power and telecommunication lines adequately from interception of data or damage.
            5.7.3 Physically separate power cables from communications cables to minimize interference. 
            5.7.4 Shield telecommunication cables to avoid information leakage due to electro-magnetic signals emanations.
            5.7.5 Consider the following controls for sensitive or critical systems:
    a. Installation of armored conduit and locked rooms or boxes at inspection and termination points.
    b. Utilization of electromagnetic shielding to protect the cables.
    c. Initiation of technical sweeps and physical inspections for unauthorized devices being attached to the cables.
    d. Controlled access to patch panels and cable rooms.
5.7 Cabling Security

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Ensure that power and telecommunications lines into information systems are underground wherever possible.
2. Protect power and telecommunication lines adequately from interception of data or damage.
3. Physically separate power cables from communications cables to minimize interference.
4. Shield telecommunication cables to avoid information leakage due to electro-magnetic signals emanations.
5. Consider the following controls for sensitive or critical systems:
1. Utilization of electromagnetic shielding to protect the cables.
2. Initiation of technical sweeps and physical inspections for unauthorized devices being attached to the cables.
3. Controlled access to patch panels and cable rooms.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2
metadata.heading: 5.7 Cabling Security

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_14
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.8 Security of Equipment Off-Premises
text:
5.8 Security of Equipment Off-Premises

<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.8.1 Establish an authorization procedure for taking information assets off-site.
            5.8.2 Ensure manufacturers' instructions for protecting equipment is adhered to at all times.
            5.8.3 Maintain the list of information assets off-site with the authorized employees and external party users.
The personnel shall:
            5.8.4 Ensure that equipment and media taken off the premises are not left unattended in public places.
5.8 Security of Equipment Off-Premises

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Establish an authorization procedure for taking information assets off-site.
2. Ensure manufacturers' instructions for protecting equipment is adhered to at all times.
3. Maintain the list of information assets off-site with the authorized employees and external party users.

The personnel shall:

1. Ensure that equipment and media taken off the premises are not left unattended in public places.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_15
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 5.9 Secure Disposal or Reuse of Equipment
text:
5.9 Secure Disposal or Reuse of Equipment

<CLIENTNAME> shall:
            5.9.1 Ensure storage media containing confidential information is physically destroyed or the information is destroyed, deleted, or overwritten using techniques to make the original information non-retrievable rather than using the standard delete or format function.
5.9 Secure Disposal or Reuse of Equipment

<CLIENTNAME> shall:

1. Ensure storage media containing confidential information is physically destroyed or the information is destroyed, deleted, or overwritten using techniques to make the original information non-retrievable rather than using the standard delete or format function.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_16
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
text:
6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Executives & Senior Management shall:
        6.1 Make available and support the required resources to drive physical and environmental protection; 
        6.2 Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of physical and environmental protection.
Admin and Facilities Team shall:
        6.3 Identify and classify security areas based on the assets and information they process or contain.   
        6.4 Establish access procedures to loading and unloading areas to restrict access to only authorized personnel.   
        6.5 Ensure that each facility’s supporting utilities are tested and protected against malfunctions.
        6.6 Document supplier's recommendations for the maintenance of equipment and make them available to maintenance personnel.   
        6.7 Keep maintenance logs for all equipment.   
        6.8 Establish processes and procedures for decommissioning and commissioning of equipment to ensure protection of sensitive data.
        6.9 Define security perimeters for every classified security level to ensure the right level of protection is applied.   
        6.10 Establish guidelines for working in secure areas.   
        6.11 Establish procedures to protect off-site equipment.   
        6.12 Establish procedures for secure disposal or re-use of equipment based on the sensitivity of stored information.   
        6.13 Ensure the right security countermeasures are adopted to protect areas that contain information and information systems.   
        6.14 Ensure that all persons wear some form of visible identification within organization’s premises.   
        6.15 Ensure that all personnel are aware of organization’s guidelines for working in secure areas.  
        6.16 Document and implement a process to authenticate all persons accessing secure areas.   
        6.17 Ensure that access to secure areas is logged.   
        6.18 Ensure that access logs are monitored and updated.   
        6.19 Control access of devices to secure areas.
Information Security shall:
        6.20 Support Physical Security and Facility Management in establishing and implementing controls and processes for physical and environmental security.
Legal shall:
        6.21 Identify all relevant and applicable laws and regulations to <CLIENTNAME> and communicate to the respective parties.
        6.22 Define standard legal clauses and terms for the contract/agreement to be signed with external parties, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
        6.23 Liaise with IS Team to define and review Information Security clauses in third-party contracts/agreements, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
        6.24 Review third-party vendor, clients and partner contracts and involve IS Team for review of clauses concerning Information Security responsibilities.
Human Capital shall:
        6.25 Make sure all personnel accessing secure areas are aware of, and accept organization’s rules and guidelines.
Information Technology shall:
        6.26 Configure equipment and systems to implement automatic protections when left unattended.  
        6.27 Maintain the list of information assets off-site with the authorized employees and external party users.
Employees, Line Managers and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.)	 shall:
        6.28 Employees and third parties (including contractors, vendors, etc.) who access organizational premises, information and information assets or use information and communications technology (ICT) systems of the organization are responsible for adhering to this Policy and all its applicable procedures.
        6.29 The following apply to all personnel, irrespective of their role within organization:
            6.29.1 Contractors/third parties shall be always escorted unless the area is explicitly designated for them with no escort requirement.   
            6.29.2 Inspect and register incoming and outgoing material in accordance with organization’s asset management procedures.   
            6.29.3 Monitor your work environmental conditions and alert in case of a potential threat.   
            6.29.4 Comply with organization’s procedures when leaving equipment unattended.   
            6.29.5 Authenticate all persons accessing secure areas.   
            6.29.6 Document the access to secure areas.   
            6.29.7 Supervise activities in secure areas.
        6.30 Make sure all personnel accessing secure areas are aware of, and accept organization’s rules and guidelines.   
        6.31 Ensure that all persons wear some form of visible identification within organization’s premises.   
        6.32 Ensure that when no longer needed, equipment is disposed of securely and records of its disposition are retained.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

| Role                                                                    | Responsibility

metadata.heading: 6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_17
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
text:
7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENTNAME>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENTNAME>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.

Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_passage_18
name: Physical and Environmental Security Policy - 8 MONITORING AND REVIEW
text:
8 MONITORING AND REVIEW

This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENTNAME> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO. 
<CLIENTNAME> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team at infosec@<clientname>.net.
8 MONITORING AND REVIEW

<CLIENTNAME> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.

Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team at infosec@<clientname>.net.
